title: Posture Assessment Trending
app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`, Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808), `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (Prisma Access), `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices` (Entra ID / Intune)
spl:
index=zt sourcetype="zt:posture"
| timechart span=1d avg(compliance_pct) as compliance by check_type
description: Endpoint posture compliance rates over time measure security improvement and identify persistent non-compliance areas.
value: Zero Trust operations teams track device posture compliance as a continuous signal over time, correlating dips with OS updates, new posture requirements, and endpoint agent issues, and feeding posture trends into access policy decisions.
implementation: Track posture assessment results over time by check type (AV, encryption, OS patch, firewall). Report on compliance improvement trends. Alert when compliance drops below target. Identify persistent non-compliance patterns.
visualization: Line chart (compliance trend by check), Bar chart (compliance by OS), Single value (overall compliance %), Table (non-compliant checks).
knownFalsePositives: Posture failures during scheduled patch cycles, OS upgrades, or after AV definition rotations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Device posture data from ZTNA/SSE platforms in `index=ztna` or `index=azure`. Zscaler ZPA/ZIA: `sourcetype=zscaler:zpa:user_activity` (includes device posture signals). CrowdStrike ZTA: `sourcetype=crowdstrike:zta` with Zero Trust Assessment scores. Microsoft Intune: `sourcetype=ms:intune:compliance`. Key fields vary: Zscaler includes `PostureProfile`, `PostureStatus`. CrowdStrike ZTA provides a 1-100 score per device.
• In Zero Trust, device posture is a continuous signal — not just a point-in-time check at login. Posture is re-evaluated throughout the session, and access can be revoked mid-session if the device becomes non-compliant.
• Create a `zt_posture_thresholds.csv` lookup: `app_tier,min_posture_score,required_checks` defining posture requirements per application tier.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify posture signal data:
```spl
index=ztna sourcetype="zscaler:zpa:user_activity" earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(PostureProfile)
| stats count by PostureProfile, PostureStatus
```

Or for CrowdStrike ZTA:
```spl
index=crowdstrike sourcetype="crowdstrike:zta" earliest=-24h
| stats avg(score) as avg_score perc95(score) as p95_score min(score) as min_score by aid
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Posture trending:
```spl
index=ztna earliest=-30d
| eval posture_pass=if(PostureStatus="Pass" OR PostureStatus="Compliant", 1, 0)
| timechart span=1d avg(posture_pass) as compliance_rate
| eval compliance_pct=round(compliance_rate*100, 1)
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends the device posture compliance rate over 30 days. In a Zero Trust model, the target should be >95%. Dips correlate with: OS updates that change default settings, new posture requirements being enabled, or endpoint protection agent issues. A sustained downtrend means the endpoint management program is falling behind.

Failed posture by check type:
```spl
index=ztna earliest=-7d
| where PostureStatus!="Pass" AND PostureStatus!="Compliant"
| stats count dc(User) as users by PostureProfile
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Check the ZTNA admin console for device posture status.
(b) Disable a posture check on a test device and verify the status change.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Compliance rate trend. Row 2 — Top failed posture checks. Row 3 — CrowdStrike ZTA score distribution.
Alert: When compliance rate drops below 90%.

Runbook:
1. **Compliance dip after OS update** — Expected. Should recover within 48h.
2. **New posture check added** — Monitor for 72h to gauge impact before enforcing.
3. **CrowdStrike ZTA score declining** — Sensor health issue. Check CrowdStrike console.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **PostureStatus always Pass** — Posture checks may be too lenient. Review posture profile configuration.
• **No posture data** — ZTNA client may not have posture module installed. Check client deployment.
grandmaExplanation: In Zero Trust, we do not just check your device once when you log in — we keep checking it all day long. We track how many devices pass these ongoing checks over time, and when fewer pass, we investigate what changed.
